vocabulary.definition: The sum of all values divided by the number of values (average)
vocabulary.visual: Mean of 2, 4, 6: add them (2+4+6=12), divide by 3 → mean = 4

vocabulary.definition: The middle value when data is ordered from least to greatest
vocabulary.visual: Data: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 → the middle (3rd) value is 5, so median = 5

vocabulary.definition: The value that appears most often in a data set
vocabulary.visual: Data: 2, 3, 3, 5, 7 → 3 appears twice (most often), so mode = 3

vocabulary.definition: A data value that is much greater or less than most other values
vocabulary.visual: Data: 10, 12, 11, 13, 50 → 50 is far from the others, so 50 is an outlier

vocabulary.definition: The shape and spread of a data set
vocabulary.visual: If most values cluster in the center with fewer at the ends, the distribution is symmetric

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the values in a data set are
vocabulary.visual: 8, 9, 10, 11 has low variability (close together); 2, 10, 25, 40 has high variability (far apart)

launch.narrative: You're the stats analyst for the school basketball team. Coach needs a report on the team's scoring: in the last 7 games they scored 42, 38, 55, 41, 38, 44, and 40 points. Which average best represents the team's typical performance?
launch.contextImage: Basketball scoreboard with stat sheets and a clipboard
noticePrompts: What are the highest and lowest scores?
Which score seems like a typical game for this team?
wonderPrompts: Will the mean and median be the same number?
How does the 55-point game affect the average?

explore.label: Order the scores on the number line, then find the mean, median, and mode. Place markers at: A = Mean, B = Median, C = Mode.

discourse.prompt: The mean (42.6 ≈ 42) is higher than the median (41). Why? Which better represents a 'typical' game?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The mean is higher than the median because the outlier score of ___ pulls the mean ___. The ___ better represents a typical game because ___.

approaching.explanation: 4 appears three times — more than any other value. Mode = 4.

approaching.instructions: Sort each step into the correct order for finding the median.

categories.label: Steps to find the median (in order)
items: Step 1: Order the data from least to greatest
Step 2: Count the total number of values
Step 3: Find the middle value (or average of two middle values)
Step 4: The middle value is the median

hints: You must order the data FIRST before finding the middle.
If there is an even number of values, average the two middle values.
approaching.label: Put the steps for finding median in the correct order.

onLevel.label: Match each measure to its definition.
Data: 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Place the mean and median on the number line.
hints: Ordered data: 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Middle value = 30. When data is symmetric, mean = median.

onLevel.stem: Data set: 4, 6, 6, 8, 10, 12. What is the median?
onLevel.explanation: With 6 values, the median is the average of the 3rd and 4th values: (6 + 8) ÷ 2 = 7.

extending.title: Find the Stats Error
extending.correctWork: Mode = 15 (appears twice, more than any other value). Mode is the most frequent value, not the largest.
hints: Mode means 'most often,' not 'most.' Which number appears the most times?
15 appears twice. No other number repeats. So the mode is 15, not 18.

extending.prompt: A basketball player scored 15, 18, 16, 42, and 14 points in 5 games. Find the mean and median. Which better represents the player's typical game? Explain how the outlier affects each measure.
extending.sentenceFrame: Mean = ___. Median = ___. The ___ better represents a typical game because the outlier (___) pulls the ___ toward it, making it ___ than most scores. The median is not affected because ___.

editableCells.answer: Both work — no outlier, data is symmetric
Median — the outlier 50 pulls the mean to 19.2, which is higher than 4 of the 5 values

connect.scenario: Your class took a math quiz. The scores were: 85, 92, 78, 88, 92, 95, 72, 88, 92, 84. The teacher says 'most students scored around 87.' A student disagrees and says 'the most common score was 92.'
connect.promptQuestion: Who is using the mean, and who is using the mode? Which is more useful here?
connect.prompt: The teacher used the ___ (about 86.6). The student used the ___ (92). The ___ is more useful here because ___.

exitTicket.stem: Data set: 4, 7, 10, 7, 12. What is the median?
